steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage opens with the narrator predicting she will succeed and then lists her speed, size advantage, and tactical skill as support. The question asks what the passage mainly does, so the correct choice must describe this prediction-plus-evidence structure.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — To compare basketball with other sports.: the passage is set entirely within one sport (basketball). No comparison between sports appears. ✗
  • Choice B — To provide details of how to play basketball.: the narrator does not explain rules or mechanics. She is justifying a personal prediction, not instructing the reader. ✗
  • Choice C — To state how players will be chosen for the basketball team.: no selection process or tryout criteria are discussed. ✗
  • Choice D — To explain why the narrator thinks she might make the basketball team.: the narrator states her prediction (‘there's just a chance that I shall make it’) and then walks through the athletic advantages that support it. This matches the passage's structure exactly. ✓
That matches Choice D — To explain why the narrator thinks she might make the basketball team..
